story.iWant: my text edits to survive layout regenerations
story.soThat: I don't have to re-enter content when I change the layout

task.subject: Implement Edit State Manager Module
task.subtask: Create function to read complete slide-state.json edits array
Create function to identify orphaned edits (selector not in new HTML)
Implement orphanedEdits array management with orphanedAt timestamp and reason field
Implement regenerationCount increment on successful save
Ensure state file write preserves all existing data (edits + orphanedEdits)

task.subject: Enhance Edit Reapplicator Logic
task.subtask: Implement data-field selector matching algorithm with lookup map
Extract field name from selector and look up in new HTML field map
If match found: replace innerHTML, mark applied, preserve in edits array
If match not found: call Edit State Manager to move to orphanedEdits

task.subject: Implement Orphan Warning System
task.subtask: After edit reapplication, collect all orphaned edits
Display warning with count and content preview if orphanedEdits count > 0
Log orphan details to status.yaml history entry
Console output should be informative but not alarming (warning, not error)

task.subject: Handle New Fields in Layout
task.subtask: After regeneration, identify new data-field values not in original
Ensure new fields have frontend-design skill's generated default content
Do NOT add empty entries to state file for new fields
Log new fields in status.yaml history entry for transparency

task.subject: Update Edit Workflow Instructions
task.subtask: Extend Phase 5B (Edit Reapplication) with orphan detection logic
Extend Phase 5C (Save and Report) for orphan warnings
Ensure state file schema matches tech spec with orphanedEdits and regenerationCount

task.subject: Testing - Edit Preservation Verification
task.subtask: Test: Create slide with 5 text edits, regenerate, verify all preserved
Test: Layout change removes field, verify orphaned edit in state file
Test: Verify warning displayed when edit is orphaned
Test: Verify orphaned edit has orphanedAt timestamp and reason
Test: Layout adds new field, verify default content used
Test: Multiple /edit operations, verify regenerationCount increments
Test: Verify state file never loses edits (even orphaned)
Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a slide has user text edits in state file, when a layout regeneration occurs via /edit, then the system reads all edits from slide-state.json
The system generates new layout HTML based on the edit instruction
The system matches data-field selectors from saved edits to elements in new HTML
For matching selectors, the saved content is injected into the new HTML elements
The updated HTML is saved, and the state file is preserved
Given a field is removed in the new layout, when no matching selector exists, then the orphaned edit is preserved in slide-state.json (not deleted)
A warning is logged when edits are orphaned
Given new fields are added in the new layout, when they have no saved edits, then they use default generated content
The regenerationCount in slide-state.json is incremented after each successful edit

doc.title: Epic 4 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Implements Edit State Manager module for slide-state.json read/write, orphaned edit handling, and regenerationCount tracking. Enhanced Edit Reapplicator iterates saved edits, matches data-field selectors, and handles orphans.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: State file pattern for preserving user edits: Read state -> generate -> reapply edits -> save. Uses data-field selectors. Never delete state entries, only add/update.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Schema: { slide: number, edits: [{selector, content}], lastModified: timestamp }. Story 4.3 extends with orphanedEdits array and regenerationCount field.

doc.title: Story 4.2: Natural Language Layout Changes
doc.snippet: Previous story implemented Phase 5A (Layout Regenerator), 5B (Edit Reapplicator), 5C (Slide Persister). Story 4.3 enhances 5B with orphan detection and 5C with orphan warnings.

file.reason: Primary file to modify - extend edit reapplication with orphan detection, orphanedEdits management, and regenerationCount tracking
Extend save phase with orphan warnings in console output and status.yaml history entries
Update workflow version and document new Story 4.3 variables (orphanedEdits, regenerationCount)
Example of current state file schema - needs orphanedEdits array and regenerationCount field added by workflow
Log orphan warnings in history entries with orphaned_edits count and field names

node.note: Story 4.3 uses only Claude Code built-in capabilities - no npm dependencies needed

constraints.constraint: Never delete state entries, only add/update. Orphaned edits preserved indefinitely.
State file edits MUST be preserved even if no matching selector exists.
Orphaned edits exist -> Preserve in state file; warn user; don't delete.
HTML slides use contenteditable on text elements with data-field attributes for selector matching.
Enhanced slide-state.json schema must include: slide, edits[], orphanedEdits[], lastModified, regenerationCount.
regenerationCount MUST increment after each successful /edit operation.
Build on existing Phase 5A, 5B, 5C implementation - enhance, don't replace.

interface.name: slide-state.json Enhanced Schema
interface.signature: { "slide": "single|{n}", "edits": [{selector, content, lastModified?}], "orphanedEdits": [{selector, content, orphanedAt, reason}], "lastModified": "ISO-8601", "regenerationCount": number }

interface.name: Edit Reapplication Algorithm
interface.signature: For each edit: match selector -> FOUND: inject content, mark applied -> NOT FOUND: move to orphanedEdits with timestamp and reason

interface.name: Orphan Warning Format
interface.signature: Warning: {N} edit(s) couldn't be matched to the new layout. - {field}: "Content preview..." (orphaned). See slide-state.json for full orphaned content.

interface.name: status.yaml History Entry
interface.signature: { action: string, timestamp: ISO-8601, preserved_edits: number, orphaned_edits: number, orphaned_fields?: string[] }

tests.standards: Per Tech Spec and Architecture: Manual verification using Frontend Test Gate checklist. Test scenarios cover edit preservation through regeneration, orphan detection and warning, state file schema validation, and multiple regeneration cycles. No automated unit tests - Claude Code workflows are tested via manual end-to-end verification.

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate in story file (notes/sprint-artifacts/4-3-edit-preservation-across-regenerations.md)
Manual browser testing with slide preview
CLI output verification for orphan warnings

ideas.idea: Create slide with 5 text edits, run /sb:edit, verify all edits loaded from slide-state.json before regeneration
Run /sb:edit with "remove the subtitle", verify new HTML generated without subtitle element
Verify matching logic: edit with selector [data-field='title'] matches element with data-field='title' in new HTML
After regeneration, inspect HTML to verify edited content injected into matching elements
After edit, verify both slide.html and slide-state.json written successfully
Request layout change that removes a field (e.g., "remove subtitle"), verify edit moved to orphanedEdits array with orphanedAt timestamp and reason
When edit is orphaned, verify warning displayed in CLI output with field name and content preview
Request layout change that adds new element (e.g., "add callout box"), verify new element has default generated content
Run multiple /edit operations, verify regenerationCount increments from 0 to 1 to 2
Test all edits orphaned: regenerate to completely different layout, verify warning and all edits preserved
Test state file missing: delete slide-state.json, run /edit, verify fresh state created
After edits, verify contenteditable and data-field attributes still present in regenerated HTML
